name: Avatar: The Last Airbender
description: The Nickelodeon animated series about a young Avatar restoring balance to a world at war

questions.q: What is the name of Aang's loyal sky bison?

source.notes: A six-legged flying bison, one of the last of his kind.

questions.q: Which of the four elements can Aang originally bend before discovering his Avatar identity?

source.notes: Aang is the last Airbender after the Fire Nation's genocide of the Air Nomads.

questions.q: What is the name of Prince Zuko's wise, tea-loving uncle?

source.notes: Known as 'The Dragon of the West'; member of the Order of the White Lotus.

questions.q: What is the name of the heavily fortified capital city of the Earth Kingdom?

source.notes: Visual design drew heavily from Beijing's Forbidden City.

questions.q: Who plays Aang in Netflix's 2024 live-action adaptation?

source.notes: Noah Ringer played Aang in M. Night Shyamalan's 2010 film.

questions.q: Which blind young earthbender joins Team Avatar in Book Two?

source.notes: Originally introduced as an underground fighter called the Blind Bandit.

questions.q: What is the name of Aang's flying lemur companion?

source.notes: Picked up at the Southern Air Temple in Book One.

questions.q: On which network did Avatar: The Last Airbender originally air starting in 2005?

source.notes: Aired Feb 21, 2005 through July 19, 2008.

questions.q: Who co-created Avatar: The Last Airbender?

source.original_a: Michael Dante DiMartino and Bryan Konietzko
source.notes: They also co-created the sequel series The Legend of Korra.

questions.q: How many seasons (called 'Books') does the original Avatar: The Last Airbender series have?

source.notes: Book One: Water, Book Two: Earth, Book Three: Fire.

questions.q: Which Star Wars alum voices Fire Lord Ozai in the animated series?

source.notes: Hamill first plays Ozai in silhouette in Book One, fully revealed in Book Three.

questions.q: Which voice actress plays Princess Azula, known for her blue flames?

source.notes: Azula is the only named firebender to produce blue flames, a sign of intense power.

questions.q: During which celestial event do firebenders gain massively amplified power?

source.notes: Its previous pass in 0 AG gave Fire Lord Sozin the power to begin the Hundred Year War.

questions.q: What is the name of the secret police that enforces order in Ba Sing Se?

source.notes: Originally founded by Avatar Kyoshi; masters of earthbending.

questions.q: Who sacrifices her life to revive the moon spirit during the Siege of the North?

source.notes: Yue had been given life by the moon spirit Tui as an infant, so she returns that gift.

questions.q: Zuko's emotionally reserved childhood friend and later love interest, known for throwing knives, is named what?

source.notes: Daughter of a Fire Nation governor; defects from Azula in Book Three.

questions.q: Toph famously pioneers which new bending discipline during the series?

source.notes: She discovers it while imprisoned in a metal box at the end of Book Two.

questions.q: Who leads the elite all-female warriors from Kyoshi Island?

source.notes: Later becomes Sokka's girlfriend.

questions.q: After voice actor Mako died between seasons, who took over voicing Uncle Iroh in Book Three?

source.notes: Mako died in July 2006; the role was dedicated to him.

questions.q: Aang first reunites with which eccentric, long-lived earthbending king in the city of Omashu?

source.notes: Bumi was Aang's childhood friend; over 110 years old by the events of the series.

questions.q: Who voiced Aang in the original animated series?

source.notes: Mitchel Musso voiced Aang in the unaired pilot; Jack DeSena voiced Sokka.

questions.q: What is the name of the guru who tries to help Aang unlock his seven chakras atop the Eastern Air Temple?

source.notes: Friend of Monk Gyatso; trains Aang in 'The Guru' near the end of Book Two.
